title: Prove the cold decision: nothing to dispatch for real, and two refusals against fixtures
action: The design rests on one cold agent reading enough external state to decide correctly, so prove the decision before proving the spawn. Record every transcript verbatim in the reference document. (a) FULL dispatch role with --dry-run against this repo's real state: docs/backlog.md has one in-flight row, PBI-043, whose slug already holds a worktree grant, so the honest answer is nothing to dispatch. The transcript must name what it examined, including the reverse index over docs/history/*/CONTEXT.md. State plainly in the document that the real dispatchable set is empty until a human runs exploring per item - a stranger must not read the empty result as a bug. (b) and (c) run at the CLASSIFIER level, not the full dispatch role, because D1 dispatchability also needs a history root and a cells store that no fixture provides: `classify-lane.mjs <id> --backlog .bee/tmp/<fixture>.md` against a fixture holding one ordinary row (expect lane_safe true, with the lane named) and a fixture holding a hard-gate row plus a row with empty text (expect lane_safe false for both, each with a distinct reason). Embed the verbatim JSON output for each fixture PBI in the document, so the verify can re-run the classifier and diff against what was recorded rather than trusting prose. Keep the fixtures under .bee/tmp/ and list their exact paths and contents in the document so the proof is reproducible.

truths: Run (a) concludes nothing is dispatchable and names what it examined, including the reverse index
The document says why the real dispatchable set is empty today, so an empty result is not read as a bug
The recorded classifier JSON for each fixture PBI matches what re-running the classifier produces
The hard-gate row and the empty row each come back lane_safe false with distinct reasons
No worktree grant is added and nothing outside the reference document and .bee/tmp/ changes

artifacts.substantive: the real-state dry run plus verbatim classifier JSON for every fixture row

prohibitions: Do not let any run create a worktree or a pane
Do not seed a fixture inside docs/backlog.md; use .bee/tmp/
Do not weaken the refusal fixtures to make them pass

verify: set -e; F=.claude/skills/herdr-orchestrating/references/dispatch-dry-run.md; test -s "$F"; grep -qi 'nothing to dispatch' "$F"; grep -qi 'hard-gate' "$F"; grep -q 'lane_safe' "$F"; node -e "const fs=require('fs');const t=fs.readFileSync('$F','utf8');const m=[...t.matchAll(/\{[^{}]*\"lane_safe\"[^{}]*\}/g)].map(x=>JSON.parse(x[0]));if(m.length<3){console.error('expected 3+ recorded classifier results, got '+m.length);process.exit(1)};if(m.filter(x=>x.lane_safe===false).length<2){console.error('expected 2+ refusals');process.exit(1)};if(!m.some(x=>x.lane_safe===true)){console.error('expected 1 pass');process.exit(1)};const r=new Set(m.filter(x=>x.lane_safe===false).map(x=>x.reason));if(r.size<2){console.error('refusal reasons are not distinct');process.exit(1)}"; node .bee/bin/bee.mjs worktree list --json | node -e "let s='';process.stdin.on('data',d=>s+=d).on('end',()=>{const g=Object.keys(JSON.parse(s).grants||{});if(g.some(k=>/fixture|throwaway|apo-/.test(k))){console.error('stray grant: '+g);process.exit(1)}})"; test -z "$(git status --porcelain -- .claude/skills .agents/skills docs/backlog.md | grep -v 'herdr-orchestrating')"; diff -q "$F" .agents/skills/herdr-orchestrating/references/dispatch-dry-run.md; echo DECISION_PROVEN
